Marquess of Bath wins High Court bid to allow his surrogate-born son to inherit part of his £157million family fortune

Ceawlin Thynn, the 8th Marquess of Bath and owner of the Longleat estate, had his second son with wife Emma, Marchioness of Bath, in 2016 via a surrogate after she suffered from a rare inflammatory condition during her first pregnancy. Henry Thynn was born to a surrogate mother in America, raising concern about his eligibility to benefit from three family trusts because of the historical definition of a legitimate child.

Joan Collins - 92 and still dressed up and attending functions

Hollywood icons Dame Joan Collins and Jane Fonda brought the glamour to the red carpet at the 79th Cannes Film Festival's opening ceremony at Palais des Festivals on Tuesday night.  Joan, 92, looked sensational in a white gown which she teamed with a diamond necklace and black gloves. The Dynasty actress was joined by her devoted husband Percy Gibson, 61, on the carpet. The leading ladies were joined on the red carpet by Demi Moore and Love Island's Maura Higgins.

Bansal-McNulty sued QPR for failing to safeguard him from the racism he suffered on loan at Crawley Town and is now seeking a £12million payout. Dinosaurs aren't extinct though. They are still around, in their billions. We call them birds.

Scientists have revealed a blow-by-blow account of what it would have been like to experience the impact of the dinosaur-killing asteroid. Around 66 million years ago, a six mile-wide space rock called Chicxulub smashed into our planet in an area of what is now the Caribbean. The impact famously wiped out the dinosaurs, who were the apex predators at the time, and caused worldwide devastation. Now, experts have detailed the sights, sounds and smells of the famous impact.

Christine McGuinness has broken her silence on her relationship with Roxxxan and confirmed to the Daily Mail she was 'single' but hinted, 'Someone might have caught my eye'. The 38-year-old said: 'I'm single. It's exciting. I'm enjoying life. The past couple of weeks have been a bit of a whirlwind. I'm struggling to keep up with my own life. Someone might have caught my eye. I just want to see how it goes. I want to do what feels natural to me. I haven't got anything planned. I'm happy and I'm excited. Dating is supposed to be fun. I feel ready to date and I haven't felt ready before. There are still things I want to work out before I have a relationship.'

All that is great about being British

For a fee, visitors can step back in time at Clovelly, a privately owned seaside village in North Devon. Known for its steep, cobbled streets, car-free roads, and picturesque bay, the settlement - home to less than 500 residents - dates to the 11th century. Historically significant, it was once a gift from William the Conqueror to his wife, Matilda of Flanders, the first woman crowned Queen of England.

Eric Clapton cut his recent Madrid concert short after being hit by a flying object. Shortly after he performed Cocaine at the Movistar Arena on May 7, an audience member threw a record at Eric, 81, near the end of the show, striking him in the chest. The Duchess of Edinburgh, 61, is widely perceived as one of the most fashionable members of the Firm and famously showcases her effortlessly chic looks with a hint of royal glamour. Sophie wed Prince Edward , the youngest of the late Queen's four children, in June 1999 - with their fairytale Windsor wedding watched by an astounding 200 million viewers across the globe. Yet while Sophie, 61, is now easily synonymous with high-end fashion, the former PR executive had a noticeably slow introduction into the image expected of a senior working royal.

A brave man from a brave generation who fought for the freedom of this country betrayed by our politicians

An unknown British soldier who gave his life during the Second World War has been identified thanks to research carried out by his great-grand-nephew. Corporal Gilbert Nay Hamilton, from Lanarkshire, took part in the invasion of Sicily, where he was killed in action during the Battle for Gerbini in July 1943. The 22-year-old received a hasty burial and, although a cross was erected showing his rank and regiment, all details of his identity were subsequently lost. In March 1944, the remains of an unidentified Corporal of the Argyll & Sutherland Highlanders were exhumed from a field grave near Gerbini and moved to the Catania War Cemetery.
  • 17 comments
  • share Unknown British soldier who died in WW2 is identified 83 years on

Amy Childs reveals her weight plummeted to under 7st after turning to 'skinny jabs' as a 'quick fix' that left her 'scared, ill and bed-bound for three days'

Amy Childs' weight has been a talking point after fans noticed she appeared much slimmer in her swimwear shots on Instagram. The TOWIE star, 35, has faced constant criticism that she has been using skinny jabs, especially after her wedding photos surfaced last month. The beautician explained that her beloved mum, Julie, had a heart attack last year which contributed to her weight loss, but she also tried skinny jabs for a brief spell.
  • 13 comments
  • share Amy Childs reveals her weight plummeted to under 7st

As an ex RAF Officer, I have often questioned the point of leaving a perfectly serviceable aircraft in mid flight. I now have an answer. Outstanding job, lads. Well done.

The Ministry of Defence released the footage, filmed from a paratrooper's helmet, capturing the daring jump by a member of the Army's 16 Air Assault Brigade Combat Team.
  • 30 comments
  • 2 videos
  • share Moment soldiers jump out of plane in aid mission for hantavirus victim

Cristiano Ronaldo's fiancee Georgina Rodriguez follows Kendall Jenner and Heidi Klum by modeling Calzedonia bikinis

Cristiano Ronaldo's fiancee Georgina Rodriguez has landed a major modeling campaign. The brunette beauty from Spain is the new face of Calzedonia's swimsuit advertisement after Heidi Klum did it last. The Saudi Arabia resident was seen in a brown and beige string bikini while posing at a white house by the ocean.
